messages.content: Is there a type of event for right click open in the new tab in React
What if I want to continue to have the default context menu but just want to add additional logic before user goes to another page?
what kind of event happens when user clicks on open link in a new tab?

When a user clicks on "Open link in new tab" in the context menu, it triggers a single-click event on the link element with the `target="_blank"` attribute. This event will trigger a new tab or window to open with the contents of the linked URL.

In React, this behavior is handled automatically by the browser when the user interacts with a link that has the `target="_blank"` attribute. You don't need to add any special event handling for this behavior in most cases. The browser will take care of opening the link in a new tab or window when the user instructs it to do so through the context menu or by using keyboard shortcuts.
can I modify Open link in new tab in the context menu event specifically?

No, the "Open link in new tab" option in the context menu is a default behavior provided by the browser and cannot be modified directly through JavaScript or React. 

The browser's context menu options are controlled by the browser itself and are not customizable through code. When a user right-clicks on a link and selects the "Open link in new tab" option from the context menu, the browser automatically opens the link in a new tab based on the `target="_blank"` attribute of the link.

However, you can create custom context menus using libraries like react-contextmenu or by defining your own context menu behavior using a combination of event listeners, state management, and custom UI components in React. But modifying or overriding the default behavior of the browser context menu options, such as "Open link in new tab", is not possible.

But whenever user clicks on Open link in new tab in the context menu he goes to another page without triggering a certain event that will log him off, I can't allow users to stay online on other pages. I need to prevent that somehow withoud disabling context menu
Does beforeunload gets triggered when the user uses context menu, clicks on open link in a new tab,  the browser opens that link in a new tab and user still is on the same page?

The `beforeunload` event is triggered when the user tries to navigate away from the current page, regardless of whether it's by closing the tab/window, typing a new URL, clicking on a link to another page, or using context menu options like "Open link in new tab".

However, the `beforeunload` event is not triggered when the user opens a link in a new tab using the "Open link in new tab" option from the context menu. This is because the user is technically not navigating away from the current page; a new tab is opened with the linked content, but the current page remains open in its original tab.

As a result, the `beforeunload` event is not fired in this scenario because the user is still on the same page in the original tab. The event is primarily intended to provide a warning or perform actions when the user is about to leave the current page entirely.
but I need to track if user has opened a new tab with a certain url to set him offline
what about applying the same logic but by using WebSocket?

but lets imagine my web socket sent this message, the user opened the other tab and I want to log him off using websocket but by checking his status on the frontend side and sending requiest to log him off from there
can you disable context menu on the whole component in React?
how to prevent rightclick mouse event?

I get a typescript error Type '{ version: string; onContextMenu: (e: any) => void; }' is not assignable to type 'IntrinsicAttributes & ISideNavigationBar'.
  Property 'onContextMenu' does not exist on type 'IntrinsicAttributes & ISideNavigationBar'
